data.body:
## Context

Teams onboarding a new project with the DSO plugin currently face three separate skills (project-setup, dev-onboarding, design-onboarding) that overlap in detection, ask rigid sequential questions, and lose context between invocations. This forces users to repeat themselves, answer questions they don't understand, and deal with false detection results (non-existent commands suggested, databases missed, wrong defaults). Exploration agents in other DSO workflows have shown similar hallucination patterns — reporting inferred findings as confirmed facts. The onboarding experience should be a single Socratic conversation that builds holistic project understanding, with detection grounded in filesystem verification, producing lasting documentation and accurate configuration as outputs.

## Success Criteria

1. A single /dso:onboarding skill replaces /dso:project-setup, /dso:dev-onboarding, and /dso:design-onboarding — invoking any old name redirects with guidance to use /dso:onboarding
2. The conversation produces a structured markdown project understanding artifact (.claude/project-understanding.md) capturing all detected and user-confirmed details about stack, architecture, design, commands, infrastructure, and enforcement preferences — usable as both a skill-to-skill context bridge and living project documentation
3. DESIGN_NOTES.md moves to .claude/design-notes.md, produced as a second output of the onboarding conversation when the project has a UI/design component; all existing references to DESIGN_NOTES.md across the codebase are updated to point to .claude/design-notes.md
4. /dso:architect-foundation (renamed from dev-onboarding) reads .claude/project-understanding.md as starting context, uses Socratic dialogue for any additional understanding it needs, and produces no duplicate questions or detection when run after onboarding
5. Detection is grounded in filesystem verification: every suggested command, tool, or capability is confirmed to exist before being presented — inferred-but-unverified findings are tagged as such and presented differently to the user
6. CI workflow configuration is contextually aware: when no GitHub Actions workflows exist, the skill offers example workflows and auto-populates subsequent CI questions from those examples if accepted; ACLI_VERSION and ACLI_SHA256 are suggested automatically
7. Ticket prefix defaults derive from the project name; database detection accounts for Docker-based databases referenced in config and code
8. The skill maintains a scratchpad file (appended to throughout the conversation) to protect against context window loss
9. At completion, /dso:onboarding offers the user the option to invoke /dso:architect-foundation

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Single-phase Socratic conversation (Option A) organized around an internal checklist of understanding areas. The agent probes freely across areas based on relevance, maintains a scratchpad for context protection, and produces all artifacts at once when confidence is high. Detection uses Chain-of-Verification: initial scan followed by independent filesystem verification of each finding, with confidence tagging (confirmed vs. inferred).
